draft.title: a music app
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a music app

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: A cool, calm, intimate mood creates trust and focus in music apps
facade.label: Midnight blue background
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward cooler palettes; may prefer evening/night listening contexts and introspective moods
facade.rejectImplies: User leans warm, bright, or daylight-oriented; prefers energetic or social listening contexts

evidence.hypothesis: A cool, calm, intimate mood creates trust and focus in music apps
evidence.implication: User gravitates toward cooler palettes; may prefer evening/night listening contexts and introspective moods

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer persistent left sidebar for album/playlist access over tab-based switching
facade.label: Sidebar navigation
facade.acceptImplies: Explore vertical hierarchy, sticky nav, minimize clicks to library. Test drawer vs permanent.
facade.rejectImplies: Explore bottom tabs or top tab bar. Test tab-based playlist switching instead.

facade.hypothesis: Users building music apps prefer high-contrast interfaces with bright accent colors for visual hierarchy and energy
facade.label: Dark mode with neon accents
facade.acceptImplies: Explore dark backgrounds with vibrant UI elements, electric blues/pinks/purples for buttons and highlights, high contrast for readability and mood
facade.rejectImplies: Avoid dark mode, or avoid bright accent colors; lean toward muted palettes, softer contrast, or lighter backgrounds

facade.hypothesis: Users expect instant playback on touch without transition delays
facade.label: Tap to play
facade.acceptImplies: Build snappy, zero-delay tap interactions; instant audio feedback; minimize animation overhead on core controls
facade.rejectImplies: Smooth transitions and choreographed animations are more important than immediate tactile response; accept slight latency for visual polish

facade.hypothesis: Users building music apps are uncertain whether to lead with metrics (plays, followers, streams) or narrative context (who listened, when, why)
facade.label: Big numbers, small context
facade.acceptImplies: The app should prioritize bold, prominent stats (play counts, listener totals) with minimal surrounding explanation—let the numbers speak for themselves.
facade.rejectImplies: The app should bury raw metrics inside richer narratives: 'Your friend just played this 47 times' instead of just showing '47 plays'.

facade.hypothesis: Users building a music app may prefer a friendly, approachable tone over corporate stiffness—testing whether voice skews toward conversational ease or professional polish
facade.label: Casual and playful
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward warm, approachable personality. Likely wants onboarding, copy, and UI microcopy to feel like a friend, not a brand. Suggests tolerance for emoji, humor, informal language.
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers measured, credible tone. May want sophisticated language, restraint in personality expression, or authority-first communication. Suggests music app should feel curated/expert rather than buddy-buddy.

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal, album-art-forward visual design vs. dense, information-rich interfaces with many visual elements
Whether user wants playback controls always visible and accessible vs. hidden/minimalist controls that prioritize content
Whether user responds to a conversational, encouraging tone vs. neutral, straightforward communication
Whether user prefers energetic, upbeat atmosphere vs. calm, focused listening environment
Whether user wants detailed song metadata and recommendations visible vs. simplified view showing only essentials
Whether user expects smooth, animated transitions between screens vs. instant, snappy navigation without flourishes

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal, album-art-forward visual design vs. dense, information-rich interfaces with many visual elements
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants playback controls always visible and accessible vs. hidden/minimalist controls that prioritize content
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to a conversational, encouraging tone vs. neutral, straightforward communication
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers energetic, upbeat atmosphere vs. calm, focused listening environment
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants detailed song metadata and recommendations visible vs. simplified view showing only essentials
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user expects smooth, animated transitions between screens vs. instant, snappy navigation without flourishes
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Warm, energetic lighting creates an inviting and emotional listening experience, contrasting with the cool palette already accepted
facade.label: Warm amber glow
facade.acceptImplies: User may prefer music apps that feel alive, social, and emotionally present—possibly more suitable for active listening or discovery contexts
facade.rejectImplies: User confirms preference for cool, calm atmospheres and may want to avoid warmth/energy in the interface, reinforcing introspective design direction

draft.title: SoundWave - Music Discovery App
draft.summary: A mobile music app for discovering, playing, and organizing your favorite tracks with a clean, warm interface. Start with home feed, now playing, and library sections.
draft.html:
SoundWave

Now Playing
Midnight Echo
Luna & The Waves
Discover
Neon Lights
Synth Dreams
Ocean Breeze
Coastal Vibes
Urban Pulse
City Sounds
